title: The One Where Mr. Heckles Dies

expressions.dialogue: **Joey**: When I first moved to the city, I **went out** a couple of times **with** this girl, really hot, great kisser, but she had the biggest Adam's apple.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: "date"보다 캐주얼하고 진지하지 않은 만남에도 쓴다. "a couple of times"와 함께 쓰면 짧고 가벼운 만남을 암시.
expressions.example: I went out with her a few times in college, but it never got serious. → 대학 때 몇 번 만났는데 진지해진 적은 없어.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: C'mon, you're going out with the guy! There's gotta be something wrong with him!

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Wait a minute! We're talking about someone that I'm going out with?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: What's going on here? You go out with tons of girls.

expressions.expression: gotta side with someone (on this one)
expressions.dialogue:
**Monica**: How many perfectly fine women are you gonna reject?
**Joey**: Hold it hold it. I **gotta side with** Chandler **on this one**.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 이번엔 ~편을 들어야 한다, ~에 동의할 수밖에 없다
expressions.nuance: 논쟁에서 특정 편을 지지할 때. "on this one"을 붙이면 평소엔 아니지만 이번만큼은이라는 뉘앙스가 살아난다.
expressions.example: I hate to say it, but I gotta side with my boss on this one. → 말하기 싫지만 이번엔 상사 편을 들어야겠어.

expressions.dialogue:
**Ross**: Uh, Joey, women don't have Adam's apples.
**Joey**: You guys are **messin' with** me, right?
**All**: Yeah.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: "Are you serious?" 대신 "Are you messing with me?"는 '장난치는 거지?'라는 의심과 당혹감이 섞인 표현. 가벼운 속임에 자주 쓰인다.
expressions.example: Wait, the meeting was cancelled? Are you messing with me? → 잠깐, 회의가 취소됐다고? 나 놀리는 거야?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: I don't know, you don't wanna mess with corn nuts. They're craaazy.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Okay, okay-okay, ah, Chloe works with that guy Issac. Issac's sister is Jasmine. And Jasmine works at that message place with Phoebe. And Phoebe's friends with Rachel. And that's the trail, I did it!

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: No! Because he's in love with the British chippy! Look, Rachel, if you go, you're just gonna mess with his head and ruin his wedding! Y'know, it's too late! You missed you're chance! I'm sorry, I know this must be really hard, it's over.

expressions.dialogue:
**Mr. Heckles**: You're stomping. It's disturbing my birds.
**Monica**: Ok, Mr. Heckles, we'll try to **keep it down**.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 주변 소음을 낮춰달라고 요청할 때. "Keep it down!"처럼 명령형으로도 자주 쓰이며 직접적이고 간결한 표현이다.
expressions.example: Can you guys keep it down? I'm on a call. → 좀 조용히 해줄 수 있어? 나 통화 중이야.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: I don't know. We're talking about whipped fish, Monica. I'm just happy I'm keeping it down, y'know?

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: We'll try to keep it down.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Could you guys please try to keep it down, we're trying to start a Boggle tournament.

expressions.dialogue:
**Mr. Treeger**: I was sweepin' yesterday. It coulda been me.
**Ross**: Sure, sweepin'. **You never know**.
**Mr. Treeger**: **You never know**.
expressions.speaker: Ross / Mr. Treeger
expressions.meaning: 모르는 일이다, 언제 무슨 일이 생길지 모른다
expressions.nuance: 예상치 못한 일이 일어났을 때 공감이나 체념을 담아 쓰는 표현. 짧지만 삶의 무상함을 담고 있다.
expressions.example: You should apply even if you think you won't get it — you never know. → 안 될 것 같아도 지원해봐, 모르는 일이잖아.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Well... you never know. How's, um.. how's the family?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Fine. Fine, alright, now you'll never know.

occ.speaker: Mr. Douglas
occ.text: I see. I guess you never really know what's goin' on inside a person's head.

expressions.dialogue: **Phoebe**: Ok, it's very faint, but I can still sense him in the building. **Go into the light**, Mr. Heckles!
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 빛 속으로 가다 — 영혼이 저세상으로 편히 떠나길 바랄 때 쓰는 표현
expressions.nuance: 미국 공포·드라마에서 자주 등장하는 상징적 표현. 이승에 미련이 남은 영혼에게 떠나라고 인도할 때 쓴다.
expressions.example: The medium closed her eyes and whispered, 'Go into the light.' → 영매는 눈을 감고 속삭였다, '빛으로 가세요.'

expressions.dialogue:
**Ross**: You don't believe in evolution?
**Phoebe**: I don't know, it's a nice story, I just think it's a little too easy.
**Ross**: Too easy?!
**Phoebe**: Yeah, **I just don't buy it**.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: "I don't believe it"보다 더 강한 거부감. 논리나 설명이 충분하지 않아 수긍할 수 없다는 뉘앙스.
expressions.example: He said he was stuck in traffic for three hours, but I don't buy it. → 3시간 동안 교통체증이었다는데 믿기지 않아.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: It kinda worked. I mean y'know, I don't know about you buy I haven't thought about our thing since all this.

expressions.dialogue:
**Ross**: You uh, you don't believe in gravity?
**Phoebe**: Ok, **don't get me started on** gravity.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 그 얘기는 꺼내지도 마, 그거 말하면 끝이 없어
expressions.nuance: 특정 주제에 대해 강한 의견이 있어서 말하기 시작하면 멈출 수 없다는 뜻. 유머러스하게도, 진지하게도 쓰인다.
expressions.example: Don't get me started on the subway system in this city. → 이 도시 지하철 얘기는 꺼내지도 마.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Don't get me started on that.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Well, the end table is wrong, The couch looks bizarre and don't even get me started on the refrigerator magnets.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Don't even get me started on yours!

expressions.dialogue:
**Chandler**: I'm gonna **end up alone**, just like he did.
**Joey**: Chandler, Heckles was a nut case.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: "end up + 상태"는 '결국 ~하게 되다'라는 패턴. 두려움이나 체념을 담아 미래를 예측할 때 쓴다.
expressions.example: If you keep pushing people away, you'll end up alone. → 계속 사람들을 밀어내면 결국 혼자 남을 거야.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, I ended up in the storage room, and not alone.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: How was I supposed to know we'd end up being friends after college, let alone you-you would be living with my sister?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: They've only been going out for a few weeks and Phoebe is completely hung up on Mike! She'll say "No", David's heart will be broken, it will be too hard for them to recover from and then Phoebe will end up alone again.

expressions.dialogue: **Monica**: You know what you want now. Most guys **don't even have a clue**.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: "don't know"보다 훨씬 강한 무지를 표현. "even"이 들어가면 '그 정도조차'라는 강조 뉘앙스가 더해진다.
expressions.example: I don't even have a clue what I want to do with my life. → 앞으로 어떻게 살고 싶은지 감조차 없어.

expressions.dialogue:
**Phoebe**: **I can't believe you caved**.
**Ross**: What?
**Phoebe**: You just abandoned your whole belief system.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 압박이나 설득에 못 이겨 자신의 입장을 포기할 때. "give in"보다 더 갑작스럽고 완전한 항복의 뉘앙스.
expressions.example: He said he'd never lend money, but he caved when she started crying. → 절대 안 빌려준다고 했는데 그녀가 울기 시작하자 굴복했어.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Marsha, these are cave people. Okay? They have issues like 'Gee, that glacier's getting kinda close.' See?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: I remember the day I got my first pay check. There was a cave in in one of the mines, and eight people were killed.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Hey, you're not him. You're you. When they were all over you to go into your father's pipe-fitting business, did you cave?

expressions.dialogue: **Phoebe**: What is this obsessive need you have to make everyone agree with you? No, **what's that all about**?
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 그게 다 무슨 의미야?, 그건 도대체 왜 그런 거야?
expressions.nuance: 누군가의 행동이나 상황의 이유를 물을 때 의아함이나 약한 비판을 담아 쓰는 표현.
expressions.example: He walked out of the meeting without a word. What was that all about? → 한마디도 없이 회의를 나가버렸어. 그게 무슨 일이야?

expressions.dialogue: **Phoebe**: I think maybe it's time you **put Ross under the microscope**.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 현미경으로 보듯 세밀하게 살핀다는 비유. 주로 비판적이거나 자성을 촉구하는 맥락에서 쓰인다.
expressions.example: Before judging others, maybe put yourself under the microscope. → 남을 판단하기 전에 자신을 먼저 들여다봐.

expressions.dialogue: (졸업앨범 글에서) "Heckles, **you crack me up** in science class. You're the funniest kid in school."
expressions.speaker: Heckles의 급우 (졸업앨범)
expressions.nuance: "you're funny"보다 훨씬 강한 표현. 웃음을 참을 수 없을 정도일 때 쓴다. 주어는 보통 사람이나 상황.
expressions.example: His impression of our professor cracks me up every time. → 교수님 흉내 낼 때마다 너무 웃겨 죽겠어.

occ.speaker: Mrs. Geller
occ.text: Oh, Jack look, there's that house paint commercial that cracks you up. [the Gellers return to watching TV and Ross goes over to Monica]

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I bet this will work! (She starts dancing and Chandler cracks up.)

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Oh, you guys, with this joke. I gotta say, I know I cracked up, but I'm not even sure I got it.

expressions.dialogue: **Monica**: isn't it amazing how much you can **touch someone's life**, without even knowing it?
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 누군가의 삶에 영향을 주다, 감동을 주다
expressions.nuance: "affect"보다 감정적이고 따뜻한 표현. 의도하지 않았어도 타인의 삶에 긍정적인 흔적을 남겼을 때 쓴다.
expressions.example: You might not realize it, but you've touched a lot of people's lives. → 모르고 있겠지만, 많은 사람들의 삶에 영향을 줬어.

expressions.dialogue: **Chandler**: I've been **holding off on** asking her out in the past, because she has an unusually large head.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 하고 싶지만 의도적으로 참거나 기다릴 때. "delay"보다 더 구어적이고 자발적인 뉘앙스.
expressions.example: We're holding off on making any announcements until the deal is finalized. → 계약이 확정될 때까지 발표를 보류하고 있어.

occ.speaker: Wendy
occ.text: Huh? Really? -- Hm, that'd be so terrible? (She gets hold of Chandler's tie, slowly moving her hands upwards on the tie, getting closer and closer to him, seductive.)

expressions.dialogue: **Chandler**: she has an unusually large head. But, I'm not gonna **let that stuff hang me up** anymore. Look at me. I'm growing.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 사소한 것에 발목 잡히다, 집착해서 앞으로 나아가지 못하다
expressions.nuance: 별것 아닌 것에 신경 쓰여 행동하지 못하는 상태. Chandler의 자기반성 대사에서 개인적 성장의 맥락으로 쓰임.
expressions.example: Don't let the small stuff hang you up — just go for it. → 사소한 것에 걸리지 마, 그냥 해봐.

expressions.dialogue: (Chandler의 내면 독백) "All right, don't **get hung up on** it, quick, quick, list five things you like about her: Nice smile, good dresser... Big head, big head, big head!"
expressions.speaker: Chandler (내면 독백)
expressions.nuance: 어떤 결점이나 문제에 과도하게 집중해 앞으로 나아가지 못할 때. "let something hang you up"과 같은 맥락이지만 더 일반적으로 쓰임.
expressions.example: Don't get hung up on what people think of you. → 남들이 어떻게 생각하는지에 집착하지 마.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Nooo! Never! I mean, we're living in the moment. God, it is so nice for once to not have to get all hung up on 'Where is this going?'

occ.speaker: Conan
occ.text: I-I heard some of you guys talking about this earlier, but sometimes there's just a word that someone has to say that you'll get hung up on. And it'll just-the way you say the word is funny to everybody else.

expressions.dialogue: **Chandler**: **Janice was my safety net**, ok? And now I have to get a snake.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 최악의 상황에서 기댈 수 있는 사람이나 수단. 관계에서 '혹시나 싶어 남겨두는 선택지'의 비유로 자주 쓰인다.
expressions.example: She always thought of her parents as a safety net, but now she had to stand on her own. → 항상 부모님이 안전망이라고 생각했는데, 이제 스스로 서야 했다.

expressions.dialogue: **Chandler**: Our trains are **on the same track**, ok? Sure, I'm coming up 30 years behind him, but the stops are all the same. Bitter Town. Aloneville. Hermit Junction.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 같은 방향으로 가고 있다, 비슷한 길을 걷고 있다
expressions.nuance: 기차 메타포로 두 사람의 삶의 궤도가 동일함을 비유. 여기서는 자신도 Heckles처럼 고독하게 끝날 것이라는 두려움을 담고 있다.
expressions.example: I feel like we're on the same track — both trying to figure out what we really want. → 우리 비슷한 길을 걷는 것 같아, 둘 다 진짜 원하는 게 뭔지 찾는 중이잖아.

expressions.dialogue: **Chandler**: I reject anyone who's crazy enough to actually go out with me, and then I **bitch about** the fact that there aren't any great women out there.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: "complain"보다 속어적이고 반복적·짜증스러운 투정의 뉘앙스. 자기 자신에 대해서도 쓸 수 있어 자기비판 장면에서 설득력이 있다.
expressions.example: Stop bitching about your job and just quit if you hate it that much. → 직장 불평 그만하고 그렇게 싫으면 그냥 그만둬.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Ya know, I just, so weird. I mean I was in there just listening to them bitch about each other and all I kept thinking about was the fourth of July.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Oh, she was a cruel, cranky, old bitch! (Ross gives her a look) (to Ross) And I'm sorry she died. Did Dad say I get the dollhouse?

occ.speaker: Mr. Geller
occ.text: (Pointing items out on the bill.) Flowers, liquor, recarpet first floor. New guest bath, landscaping. I'm paying to remodel this guys house. (Angrily gets up.) I'm going to give that son on a bitch, a piece of my mind.

expressions.dialogue: **Chandler**: If I'm gonna be an old, lonely man, I'm gonna **need a thing**, you know, a hook, like that guy on the subway who eats his own face.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: "a thing"과 "a hook"은 여기서 사람을 정의하는 독특한 특성이나 개성을 뜻함. "have a thing" 형태로도 자주 쓰인다.
expressions.example: Every great performer has a thing. What's yours? → 훌륭한 퍼포머는 다 자기만의 특기가 있어. 넌 뭔데?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Oh, uh, that would be mine. See, I wrote a note to myself, and then I realised I didn't need it, so I balled it up and... (sees that Monica is glaring at him) ...now I wish I was dead.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Come on, Ross, you gotta get back in the game here, ok? The Rachel thing's not happening, your ex-wife is a lesbian-I don't think we need a third...

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: (to the nurse) Hi, (tiny laugh) um, I'm gonna need a new set of (tiny laugh) these forms (tiny laugh).

expressions.dialogue:
**Rachel**: Have you ever seen so much crap?
**Chandler**: Actually, I think this apartment **sullies the good name of** crap.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 원래 진지한 표현이지만 Chandler는 "crap"(잡동사니)에 붙여 역설적 유머를 만든다. 과장된 폄하에 위트 있게 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: This coffee is so bad it sullies the good name of coffee. → 이 커피는 너무 형편없어서 커피라는 이름에 먹칠을 하고 있어.

expressions.dialogue: **Chandler**: ...she has an unusually large head. But, I'm not gonna let that stuff hang me up anymore. Look at me. **I'm growing**.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 자기 자신이 변화하고 있다는 만족감을 표현. 여기서는 Chandler가 사소한 결점에 집착하지 않겠다며 스스로를 칭찬하는 셀프-아이러니로 쓰인다.
expressions.example: I actually apologized first this time. Look at me, I'm growing. → 이번엔 내가 먼저 사과했어. 봐봐, 나 성장하고 있잖아.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: (entering from her bedroom carrying a present) Ok, these were unbelievably expensive, and I know he's gonna grow out of them in like, 20 minutes, but I couldn't resist. (There a little pair of Nike shoes.)

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: I just can't believe this is happening. I mean, when I was little, everybody's parents were getting divorced. I just figured as a grownup I wouldn't have to worry about this.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: I'm just, I'm, I'm, I'm used to playing for grown-ups. Ya know, they just, grown-ups drink their coffee and do their grown-up thing, ya know, and kids listen. This is a huge responsibility. What? Are you gonna kiss me?

expressions.dialogue:
**Joey**: Chandler, **Heckles was a nut case**.
**Chandler**: Our trains are on the same track, ok?
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: "crazy person"의 구어 표현. 약간의 걱정이나 애정 섞인 어조로도 쓰이며, 상황에 따라 가볍게도 강하게도 쓰인다.
expressions.example: Don't listen to him — he's a total nut case. → 그 사람 말 신경 쓰지 마, 완전 이상한 사람이야.
